name: La'ie Falls Trail
tagline: A muddy, jungly adventure to a secluded North Shore waterfall most visitors never hear about.
parking: Limited parking near the trailhead off Poohaili Street in La'ie — respect the residential neighborhood
bestTime: After light rain for waterfall flow, but not during or right after heavy rain
talkStory: The trail follows an old irrigation ditch route built by plantation workers to channel water from Ko'olau streams to sugar cane fields in La'ie below. La'ie is deeply significant as a center of Hawaiian cultural heritage, later becoming the site of the Polynesian Cultural Center. The 25-foot falls drop into a jungle pool few tourists ever reach. Access has been restricted at times — verify current status before going. Perpetually muddy.
